subtitle: A wilderness beach at the end of an 11-mile cliff-edge trail on the Nā Pali Coast of Kauaʻi, walled by fluted green pali over 2,000 feet high — reachable only on foot or by sea, and far more than the empty paradise the brochures sell.
spike_statement: The beach at the end of one of the world's most dangerous hikes — an 11-mile Nā Pali cliff trail guarding a valley that was a Hawaiian home and a site of anti-colonial resistance, not empty paradise.

levers.photo_tone: towering emerald fluted cliffs dropping straight to a wild empty beach, a waterfall at the far end, cold Pacific surf, deep tropical greens and no development anywhere
